title: OSF Recipe master-data descriptor — next/recipes/ (GitHub-managed ProductDefinition binding) — v2.0
description: Meta-schema for one recipe file under next/recipes/. A recipe is the GitHub-managed master data behind the constraint `valueFrom: recipe:<param>` hook (ISA-95 Definition-Leg / ProductDefinition SOLL). One file per recipe, owned by Process Engineering, fetched AT RUNTIME by the it-evaluator GithubRecipeSource (push-to-GitHub goes live, no redeploy). Each file declares a `match` (article x equipment x setup binding axes) and `values` (recipe:<param> -> SpecValue) that the source aggregates into bindings. The `parameters[]` array is optional human documentation (SOLL/tolerance/unit) and is NOT consumed by the resolver. Deliberately conservative: it pins the spine the resolver reads (recipeId, version, match, values key namespace) and leaves the documentation block open.

version.description: Semantic version; bump on any band/value change.

owner.description: Accountable role/team for this recipe (e.g. 'Process Engineering').

profileRef.description: SMProfile the constrained entity uses (documentation; the resolver keys on match, not profileRef).

match.description: Binding axes (a subset of {article, equipment, setup}). An empty match is the catch-all default. The FIRST recipe whose match is a subset of the running entity's key wins per ref.

values.description: recipe:<param> -> SpecValue map. Keys MUST be the reserved 'recipe:<param>' refs the constraints address. A [lo,hi] number tuple feeds op=between bands; a scalar feeds eq/lte/gte rules; a two-tier object { warn:[lo,hi], action:[lo,hi] } feeds op=within_limits control limits (warn = Warngrenze / inner band, action = Eingriffsgrenze / outer band; warn must lie inside action).

parameters.description: Optional human documentation of the full recipe (SOLL/tolerance/unit, monitored flag). NOT consumed by the resolver.

toleranceSource.description: CAPT-TRUTH-ZUG3 — WHERE each band in `values` comes from. Keyed by the SAME recipe:<param> refs, so the provenance sits next to the band it governs (the band itself must stay a bare [lo,hi] tuple: it feeds op=between directly). WHY THIS IS REQUIRED: without it, a rule violation is UNDECIDABLE. Measured on sgm-004 (2026-07-11): recipe_part_mass_band = [10.20, 10.45] fired 58,796 times and was auto-cleared 58,796 times. The process is centred at 10.400 g with sigma 0.0839 — Cp 0.50 / Cpk 0.20, i.e. the band is HALF as wide as the process and cannot be held no matter how well it is centred. Is that a wrong RULE (widen the band, fix_charter) or an incapable PROCESS (fix_reality)? The measurements cannot say. Only the ORIGIN of the number can: a customer drawing may not be widened; a process estimate may. 'unknown' is the honest answer when nobody recorded it — and it makes every charter change from that band provably ungrounded, instead of a confident guess.

additionalProperties.description: drawing / customer_spec / norm  -> BINDING. The band may not be widened; a violation means the process is not capable (fix_reality). process_estimate               -> someone set it. It may be corrected (fix_charter). unknown                        -> nobody recorded where it came from. Neither verdict is grounded; the honest output is an evidence gap: 'bind the tolerance source'.

capability.description:
CAPT-TRUTH-ZUG3.7 — the capability a band DEMANDS, per `recipe:<param>` ref. This is Process-Engineering POLICY and therefore belongs in the SSOT, never as a constant in code (the hard-coded CPK_UNHOLDABLE = 1.33 was deleted for exactly this reason). It decomposes the customer's questions into independent numbers:
  Cp = (hi-lo) / 6*sigma        — is the band holdable by this process AT ALL?  -> the RECIPE
  Ca = |mean-soll| / ((hi-lo)/2) — does the process HIT the nominal?             -> the MACHINE
  Cpk ~= Cp * (1 - Ca)          — the consequence of both; reported, never judged on.
A band may be perfectly holdable (Cp high) while the machine misses the nominal (Ca high), and vice versa — which is exactly why one number cannot answer both questions. Measured on sgm-004: partMass_g runs at 10.400 g against a nominal of 10.35 with sigma 0.084 — a band wide enough to be holdable still leaves Ca = 0.15, i.e. the MACHINE deviates while the RECIPE is fine. No Cpk alone could have said that.
CAPT-SSOT / CAPT-STAT-EDGE (2026-07-12) add the PRECONDITION of all three: `max_stationarity_ratio`. Cp, Ca and Cpk are only defined for ONE stationary process. Over a sample that mixes states (heat-up, shift end, mould change), sigma is not the process's noise but the distance between its states — the numbers still compute, and they are lies. The ratio below decides whether a capability verdict may be issued at all.

cp_min.description: Minimum Cp. Below it the BAND cannot be held by this process, however well the machine is centred -> the RECIPE deviates. Industry floor: 1.33.

ca_max.description: Maximum Ca (off-centring, as a fraction of the half-band). Above it the MACHINE is not hitting the nominal -> the MACHINE deviates. Industry ceiling: 0.125.

max_stationarity_ratio.description:
STATIONARITY GATE — the precondition for cp_min/ca_max, not a fourth verdict. The consumer (edge/it-evaluator) computes, on the SAME gated sample it would compute Cp on:
  sigma_short = mean(|x_i - x_(i-1)|) / 1.128   (moving range of consecutive samples, d2 for n=2 — short-term/within noise)
  sigma_total = stddev(x)                        (long-term/overall spread)
  stationarity_ratio = sigma_total / sigma_short
One stationary process gives ratio ~= 1.0 (both estimate the same sigma). A ratio far above 1 means the sample contains SEVERAL states, and sigma_total is measuring the distance between them, not the process noise -> Cp/Ca/Cpk are arithmetically valid and semantically meaningless.
ABOVE this threshold the consumer MUST WITHHOLD the capability verdict and emit an evidence gap ('sample is not stationary — gate or split it'). It must NOT report a low Cp as an incapable process, and it must NOT silently drop the sample.
Measured on sgm-004 (2026-07-12, ungated series): mouldTempC 156.4 · peak/hold pressure 3.3 · partMass_g, hotrunnerTempC, cushionMm ~= 1.0. The threshold 2.0 separates these cleanly and is the pilot policy; it is a POLICY number, editable here without a release — which is the whole point of it living in the recipe. Must be > 1: a threshold of 1.0 or below would reject even a perfectly stationary sample.
